channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate Republicans open to DOGE access to IRS but urge guardrails
item.description: Senate Republicans on Tuesday said they are open to the idea of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) accessing the IRS’s sensitive taxpayer information, as long as there are guardrails in place.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Tensions rise between Trump and Zelenskyy over the Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday called Volodymyr Zelenskyy a “dictator without elections,” lashing out after the Ukrainian president said Trump was being influenced by Russian disinformation as he moves to end the Kremlin’s war against Ukraine on terms that Kyiv says are too favorable to Moscow.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Kelly Loeffler, former Georgia senator, to lead Small Business Administration
item.description: The U.S. Senate has confirmed Kelly Loeffler, a Georgia businesswoman and former senator, to lead the Small Business Administration.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Green New Headache: Dems flee gov’s green ‘Energy Master Plan’ as election approaches
item.description: Former NJ state Sen. Steve Sweeney called for an immediate halt to Gov. Phil Murphy's Energy Master Plan, citing cost concerns ahead of spiking utility rates.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Americans' confidence in air travel safety dips slightly after Washington plane crash: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Americans' confidence in air travel and the federal agencies tasked with maintaining air safety has slipped a little from last year, following a recent crash in Washington, according to a new poll, but most still believe air transportation is generally safe.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Four Florida sheriffs tapped for council advising new state immigration enforcement board
item.description: Four sheriffs have been appointed to a council advising Florida's newly created immigration enforcement board, which will ensure federal immigration laws are enforced in the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration halts support for representing unaccompanied children in immigration court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday stopped support for legal representation in immigration court for children who enter the United States alone, a setback for those fighting deportation who can't afford a lawyer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate GOP pushes ahead with budget bill that funds Trump's mass deportations and border wall
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Republicans pushed ahead late Tuesday on a scaled-back budget bill, a $340 billion package to give the Trump administration money for mass deportations and other priorities, as Democrats prepare a counter-campaign against the onslaught of actions coming from the White House.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE is getting lists of the military's probationary workers from the Pentagon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Department of Government Efficiency staffers were at the Pentagon on Tuesday and receiving lists of the military’s probationary employees, U.S. officials said.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fentanyl on coroners' radar long before Nevada lawmakers took notice
item.description: The coroner's office knows what's happening in the community long before lawmakers do. That became clear during a discussion of deaths on Nevada's roads in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs executive order asking for recommendations on lowering IVF costs
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order directing federal agencies to find ways to reduce the high cost of in vitro fertilization (IVF) treatment.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Retail recreational marijuana, take two. Cannabis management leader speaks to rollout
item.description: On Tuesday, the Office of Cannabis Management started accepting applications for licenses to grow and sell marijuana — for a second time. Eric Taubel, interim director, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. and Russia discuss ending Ukraine war, without Kyiv
item.description: The talks build on last week’s phone call between President Trump and Russian leader Vladimir Putin during which the two leaders agreed to work together toward a negotiated end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Hochul heads to NYC for meetings after 4 deputy mayors resign
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York Gov. Kathy Hochul is holding meetings on Tuesday in New York City as calls grow for Mayor Eric Adams to resign from his position. She’s set to meet with City Comptroller Brad Lander, City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams, Rev.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: 35% of people will never support Trump, DOGE
item.description: "I want this; 80% of the American people are with me. The Trump haters don't want it," O'Reilly said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a January fatal midair collision at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former Vice President Kamala Harris to receive NAACP's Chairman's Award
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris will receive the NAACP's Chairman's Award during the civil rights organization’s Image Awards ceremony this weekend. The Chairman's Award honors individuals who excel in public service and leverage their unique platforms to ignite and drive meaningful change.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Protesters to demand Gov. Hochul remove NYC Mayor Adams from office
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Protesters are expected to descend upon City Hall on Monday to demand Gov. Kathy Hochul remove New York City Mayor Eric Adams from office.
item.dc:creator: Justin Finch, PIX 11

item.title: PIX on Politics Panel breaks down Adams-DOJ controversy
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It’s been a politically charged week for Mayor Eric Adams after the Department of Justice ordered prosecutors to drop a federal corruption case against him.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Florida passes immigration bill, repealing in-state tuition for undocumented students
item.description: The third and final day of the special session concluded with the passage of an immigration bill, one that sparked significant debate among lawmakers in both the House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada joins lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford joined more than a dozen other attorneys general Thursday in filing a lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Denver Public Schools sues DHS over policy allowing ICE in schools
item.description: Denver Public Schools (DPS) sued the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) on Wednesday over its new Trump administration policy allowing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to conduct raids in schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Kamala Harris would be Democratic frontrunner for California governor in 2026, poll suggests
item.description: Former Vice President Harris would be the Democratic frontrunner for governor of California if she jumped into the Golden State’s 2026 race to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom (D), new polling shows.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WFLA, NBC
